claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for my son Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you some questions about your situation:

**Do you currently live in the UK with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I live abroad with Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific rules that may apply.

**Which country are you currently living in with Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Living in Spain may affect your eligibility, as Child Benefit eligibility can depend on your specific circumstances when abroad.

**Are you or have you been a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces), or are you working for an international organization?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or diplomat, and I don't work for an international organization.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

**Do you or Alex have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a UK national?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, no.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**Are you a UK national?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a UK national.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also ask:

**Are you planning to move back to the UK, or is there any other connection you have to the UK (such as paying UK National Insurance or taxes)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not planning to move back to the UK at the moment, and I don't have any connection like paying UK National Insurance or taxes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

While Alex is 8 years old and meets the age requirement (under 16), the main issue is that **you need to live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit**. Since you're currently living in Spain with Alex and:

- You're not a UK national
- You don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You're not a Crown servant (diplomat or member of HM Forces)
- You have no ongoing connection to the UK (such as paying UK National Insurance or taxes)

You don't meet the residency requirements or qualify for any of the exceptions that would allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK with Alex - you may be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The claimant is living in Spain with Alex and is not a UK national, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, is not a Crown servant, and has no ongoing connection to the UK such as paying National Insurance or taxes. The guidance states that you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, but the claimant does not fall into these exceptions. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is living abroad in Spain with their 8-year-old son Alex. While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement. The claimant is not a UK national, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, is not a Crown servant or diplomat, and has no ongoing connection to the UK. Without meeting the residency requirements or qualifying for any of the exceptions for those living abroad, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement. The claimant is not a UK national, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, is not a Crown servant or diplomat, and has no ongoing connection to the UK. Without meeting the residency requirements or qualifying for any of the exceptions for those living abroad, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
